title: Schizachyrium thollonii
taxonomy: Schizachyrium thollonii is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a type of grass that belongs to the genus Schizachyrium. This genus is further classified under the tribe Andropogoneae. Within the family Poaceae, Schizachyrium thollonii falls under the subfamily Panicoideae. The classification of Schizachyrium thollonii provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other grass species.
uses: Schizachyrium thollonii has been reported to have medicinal properties, primarily in traditional medicine. Although specific uses are limited, it is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. In some African cultures, the plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever and digestive issues. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of its medicinal properties. Traditional medicine practitioners continue to use Schizachyrium thollonii, but its applications are not widely documented or studied.

constituents: Schizachyrium thollonii contains various active constituents, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. These compounds are responsible for its medicinal properties and potential health benefits. The plant's flavonoids may possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, while its phenolic acids could have antimicrobial and anti-cancer activities.
